candidates.snippet:
noiseTier: "precise" as const,
  slug: "tf-module-unpinned",
  description: "Terraform module references a remote git/github source without a commit-SHA pin",
  filePatterns: ["**/*.tf"],
  match(content) {

findings.title: Single-line Terraform module blocks are not evaluated
findings.description: The matcher enters module-tracking mode when it sees a line like `module "x" {`, then immediately continues to the next line. Because it skips parsing the opening line, a single-line block such as `module "x" { source = "github.com/org/mod" }` never records the source or closing brace and therefore is not evaluated. This can cause the scanner to miss unpinned remote Terraform modules written on one line. This is a scanner correctness bug, not an exploitable vulnerability in deepsec itself.
findings.recommendation: Process the module declaration line after entering module mode, or initialize depth by counting braces on the current line and extract `source` from that same line before deciding whether the block has closed.

revalidation.reasoning: The current matcher handles the single-line module case described in the finding. When it sees a module declaration, it sets inModule=true and depth=0, but it does not continue to the next line; it immediately processes the same line. The per-character loop counts both the opening and closing braces on that line, so a block like module "x" { source = "github.com/org/mod" } reaches depth <= 0 during the same iteration and is evaluated. The source extraction regex is also no longer anchored to the beginning of the line; it uses \bsource\s*=\s*"([^"]+)" so it can find source assignments after the opening brace. The version check uses the same anywhere-on-line approach, so single-line registry modules with version are handled too. For an unpinned remote GitHub source on one line, looksRemote is true, isPinned is false, okPinned is false, and the matcher records a hit at the module start line. This is a scanner correctness fix rather than a security boundary issue in deepsec itself, and the current code removes the false negative described.
